title: Glochidion podocarpum
uses: Glochidion podocarpum, a plant native to certain regions, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. taxonomy: Glochidion podocarpum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Glochidion, which consists of species of small trees and shrubs. This species is native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. Glochidion podocarpum is a member of the order Malpighiales, which includes various families of flowering plants. The classification of Glochidion podocarpum is further detailed in the binomial nomenclature system as Glochidion podocarpum.

intro: Glochidion podocarpum, also known as the Indian mulberry, is a medicinal plant belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly found in tropical forests across Asia.
